title: Neonauclea fagifolia
intro: Neonauclea fagifolia, also known as the beechleaf neonauclea, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The beechleaf neonauclea has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Neonauclea fagifolia is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Neonauclea fagifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Neonauclea, which is a group of plants endemic to the Pacific Islands. The species is further classified into the subfamily Rubioideae, a large and diverse group within the Rubiaceae family. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. Neonauclea fagifolia is a member of the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that includes a wide range of species.
